qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JavaScript code execution via browser steps allows arbitrary code injection in browser context
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns browser-step JavaScript passed to page.evaluate() in changedetectionio/browser_steps/browser_steps.py, not jq or jqraw include filters in changedetectionio/html_tools.py reaching the jq env builtin.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded request body in import endpoint allows denial of service via memory exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns request.get_data() on POST /api/v1/import in changedetectionio/api/Import.py causing memory exhaustion, not jq or jqraw include filters disclosing process environment variables.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is environment-variable disclosure through jq: and jqraw: include filters in changedetectionio/html_tools.py where jq.compile(...).input(...).all() permits the jq env builtin. The qualifying findings instead cover browser-step page.evaluate() JavaScript execution and unbounded /api/v1/import request-body memory consumption.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-58r7-4wr5-hfx8 names changedetectionio/html_tools.py lines 380-388 and the jq env builtin in jq:/jqraw: include filters as the vulnerable path.
vulnerability_folder_1 reports changedetectionio/browser_steps/browser_steps.py:176-180 action_execute_js passing user-controlled JavaScript to page.evaluate().
vulnerability_folder_2 reports changedetectionio/api/Import.py:179 request.get_data().decode('utf8') reading unbounded request bodies.
No qualifying vulnerability report mentions changedetectionio/html_tools.py, jq:, jqraw:, include_filters, or the jq env builtin source-to-sink condition.
